trigger_reason: The current playbooks are too high-level and do not mandate explicit inclusion of evidence references or specific forensic artifacts, leading to 'lazy' investigations that rely on general patterns rather than granular incident proof.
improvements: Mandate specific sub-technique identification (e.g., T1078.002) for all MITRE mappings.
Require affirmative evidence for negative findings: Any check for persistence/lateral movement must list the specific artifacts reviewed (e.g., 'Checked WMI Event Consumers: No hits').
Add a mandatory 'Forensic Artifacts' section for PowerShell-related incidents, specifically requiring analysis of memory strings or script block logging (Event ID 4104).

checklist.note: Reconstruct chronological sequence; must link each event to its unique log timestamp and record ID.
Extract all indicators and mandate cross-reference against Geo/ASN reputation services.
Map to MITRE ATT&CK sub-techniques (e.g., T1059.001) with justification based on observable behavior.
For PowerShell/Scripting: Review memory strings or script block logs (Event ID 4104) to identify intent and de-obfuscated commands.
Verify lateral movement; must document specific artifacts checked (e.g., RDP logs, WMI, SMB traffic) even if result is negative.
Verify persistence; must document check of Run keys, Scheduled Tasks, WMI, and Services with evidence timestamp.
Assess data volume and destination; calculate byte count and categorize destination ASN reputation.
